text.title: In-Memory Application Cache (L1 with lru_cache + TTLCache + WarmableCache) Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every In-Memory Application Cache (L1 with lru_cache + TTLCache + WarmableCache) application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: `functools.lru_cache` is for pure deterministic functions only — never wrap functions with I/O.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Python docs + bugs.python.org issue history. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.
Rule rooted in source: cachetools docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Every cache MUST have a `maxsize` — never `maxsize=None` in production.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Memory-safety reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Cache keys predictable at startup MUST be preloaded via `WarmableCache.warm()` to avoid first-request latency spikes.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Performance audit reports. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Every L1 cache MUST emit `hits`, `misses`, `evictions` counters on a per-cache label.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: OpenTelemetry semantic conventions for cache. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: L1 cache MUST NOT be relied on for cross-worker consistency; use Redis for that.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Distributed-systems reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Functions that depend on mutable config MUST register a `cache.clear()` hook on config reload.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Internal feature-flag postmortems. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
